title: Broken SSL in the nested amd64 containers on Mac M1 inside arm64 container
body:
### Issue Description

On the Mac with M1 chip a nested amd64 podman (or buildah) container inside the arm64 podman container has broken SSL. This makes it impossible to build amd64 images on the arm64 machines.


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1. On Mac with M1 chip run the command 
```bash
podman run --platform linux/arm64/v8 --privileged --rm quay.io/podman/stable podman run --platform linux/amd64 --privileged ubi8 yum update -y
```

### Describe the results you received

Output:
```bash
Resolved "ubi8" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Checking if image destination supports signatures
Copying blob sha256:6208c5a2e205726f3a2cd42a392c5e4f05256850d13197a711000c4021ede87b
Copying config sha256:768688a189716f9aef8d33a9eef4209f57dc2e66e9cb5fc3b8862940f314b9bc
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Updating Subscription Management repositories.
Unable to read consumer identity

This system is not registered with an entitlement server. You can use subscription-manager to register.

Red Hat Universal Base Image 8 (RPMs) - BaseOS  0.0  B/s |   0  B     00:00
Errors during downloading metadata for repository 'ubi-8-baseos-rpms':
  - Curl error (35): SSL connect error for https://cdn-ubi.redhat.com/content/public/ubi/dist/ubi8/8/x86_64/baseos/os/repodata/repomd.xml [error:0407008A:rsa routines:RSA_padding_check_PKCS1_type_1:invalid padding]
Error: Failed to download metadata for repo 'ubi-8-baseos-rpms': Cannot download repomd.xml: Cannot download repodata/repomd.xml: All mirrors were tried
```

The `yum update` command inside the nested amd64 container is failed due to broken SSL.

### Describe the results you expected

If we run the same with the nested arm64 container it works fine:

```bash
podman run --platform linux/arm64/v8 --privileged --rm quay.io/podman/stable podman run --platform linux/arm64/v8 --privileged ubi8 yum update -y
Resolved "ubi8" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull registry.access.redhat.com/ubi8:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Checking if image destination supports signatures
Copying blob sha256:0abb68531ec6cd91f887b3384722ee0c64e3412cddfaa0e16f33bd4ec53cbd0f
Copying config sha256:f867900263f9f313ff5f796da097647e93c37adeba9833133ea0955e3edbf3e8
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Updating Subscription Management repositories.
Unable to read consumer identity

This system is not registered with an entitlement server. You can use subscription-manager to register.

Red Hat Universal Base Image 8 (RPMs) - BaseOS  701 kB/s | 609 kB     00:00
Red Hat Universal Base Image 8 (RPMs) - AppStre  12 MB/s | 2.9 MB     00:00
Red Hat Universal Base Image 8 (RPMs) - CodeRea  33 kB/s |  24 kB     00:00
Dependencies resolved.
================================================================================
 Package          Architecture Version             Repository              Size
================================================================================
Upgrading:
 libsemanage      aarch64      2.9-9.el8_6         ubi-8-baseos-rpms      164 k
 tzdata           noarch       2023c-1.el8         ubi-8-baseos-rpms      477 k

Transaction Summary
================================================================================
Upgrade  2 Packages

Total download size: 640 k
Downloading Packages:
(1/2): libsemanage-2.9-9.el8_6.aarch64.rpm      3.2 MB/s | 164 kB     00:00
(2/2): tzdata-2023c-1.el8.noarch.rpm            6.6 MB/s | 477 kB     00:00
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Total                                           8.2 MB/s | 640 kB     00:00
Running transaction check
Transaction check succeeded.
Running transaction test
Transaction test succeeded.
Running transaction
  Preparing        :                                                        1/1
  Upgrading        : libsemanage-2.9-9.el8_6.aarch64                        1/4
  Upgrading        : tzdata-2023c-1.el8.noarch                              2/4
  Cleanup          : tzdata-2022g-1.el8.noarch                              3/4
  Cleanup          : libsemanage-2.9-9.el8.aarch64                          4/4
  Running scriptlet: libsemanage-2.9-9.el8.aarch64                          4/4
  Verifying        : tzdata-2023c-1.el8.noarch                              1/4
  Verifying        : tzdata-2022g-1.el8.noarch                              2/4
  Verifying        : libsemanage-2.9-9.el8_6.aarch64                        3/4
  Verifying        : libsemanage-2.9-9.el8.aarch64                          4/4
Installed products updated.

Upgraded:
  libsemanage-2.9-9.el8_6.aarch64           tzdata-2023c-1.el8.noarch

Complete!
```

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body:
I'm not sure if it only in nested contexts:

Works fine:
```
podman run -it --platform linux/arm64 python:3.9-slim-bullseye bash -c "pip install requests && python -c 'import requests; requests.get(\"https://valid-isrgrootx1.letsencrypt.org\")'"
```

Broken:
```
podman run -it --platform linux/amd64 python:3.9-slim-bullseye bash -c "pip install requests && python -c 'import requests; requests.get(\"https://valid-isrgrootx1.letsencrypt.org\")'"

...
WARNING: Retrying (Retry(total=4, connect=None, read=None, redirect=None, status=None)) after connection broken by 'SSLError(SSLCertVerificationError(1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: certificate signature failure (_ssl.c:1129)'))': /simple/requests/
WARNING: Retrying (Retry(total=3, connect=None, read=None, redirect=None, status=None)) after connection broken by 'SSLError(SSLCertVerificationError(1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: certificate signature failure (_ssl.c:1129)'))': /simple/requests/
WARNING: Retrying (Retry(total=2, connect=None, read=None, redirect=None, status=None)) after connection broken by 'SSLError(SSLCertVerificationError(1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: certificate signature failure (_ssl.c:1129)'))': /simple/requests/
WARNING: Retrying (Retry(total=1, connect=None, read=None, redirect=None, status=None)) after connection broken by 'SSLError(SSLCertVerificationError(1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: certificate signature failure (_ssl.c:1129)'))': /simple/requests/
WARNING: Retrying (Retry(total=0, connect=None, read=None, redirect=None, status=None)) after connection broken by 'SSLError(SSLCertVerificationError(1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: certificate signature failure (_ssl.c:1129)'))': /simple/requests/
Could not fetch URL https://pypi.org/simple/requests/: There was a problem confirming the ssl certificate: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='pypi.org', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /simple/requests/ (Caused by SSLError(SSLCertVerificationError(1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: certificate signature failure (_ssl.c:1129)'))) - skipping
ERROR: Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement requests (from versions: none)
ERROR: No matching distribution found for requests
Could not fetch URL https://pypi.org/simple/pip/: There was a problem confirming the ssl certificate: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='pypi.org', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /simple/pip/ (Caused by SSLError(SSLCertVerificationError(1, '[SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate verify failed: certificate signature failure (_ssl.c:1129)'))) - skipping

```

Yes this looks like a regression qemu-user-static, there isn't really anything we can do about it.
So far you can do `podman machine init --image stable` (#18280 and #18271) but the stable coreos stream will also receive the update at some point. I think it is usually two weeks behind.

cc @baude @ashley-cui

Thank you for the feedback. `podman machine init --image stable` does help for now 👍 
So let's hope this gets sorted out before the changes hit the stable channel then.

I was able to init the machine from one of the previous images and install qemu-user-static on it:

```
pod machine init --image-path https://builds.coreos.fedoraproject.org/prod/streams/stable/builds/37.20230122.3.0/aarch64/fedora-coreos-37.20230122.3.0-qemu.aarch64.qcow2.xz
podman machine start
podman machine ssh sudo rpm-ostree install qemu-user-static
podman machine stop
podman machine start
```
The working version there is: `qemu-user-static-7.0.0-15.fc37.aarch64`

> So far you can do `podman machine init --image stable`

Thank you!

FWIW, on my local installation of podman 4.5.0 on macOS Ventura 13.3.1, the parameter was `--image-path` instead of `--image`

Given this is apparently fixed in qemu-stable 7.2.2, could we get that version or just the commit fixing this specific bug applied to qemu-user-static?
Thanks!
Podman depends on the latest qemu, and the most recent qemu version is 8.0. If you go and grab the updated qemu, this should work correctly. Closing as completed.

I installed latest podman (4.5.0), and even went through and removed all older versions of qemu and still have this issue. Not sure what else we are supposed to do to fix this issue, other than using the stable image like suggested earlier. I also uninstalled podman desktop just to make sure that wasn't potentially impacting anything.

Works fine when using the stable image workaround, at least for now.

It all depends on the qemu-user-static version inside the VM image which is just a standard fedora coreos image. 

So far fedora 38 uses qemu v7.2.1 (https://src.fedoraproject.org/rpms/qemu), so someone would need to ask the fedora package maintainer to update that package to 7.2.2 assuming that the prior comment is correct that it was fixed there.

it feels like the situation of OpenJDK in Docker Hub -- it takes a long time for a fix to be approved in the Debian distribution and then for the docker image to be pushed. The major version change (qemu 8.x) might be difficult to be approved for F38, while it is uncertain whether 7.2.x contains the fix. Is there a ticket in either RHBZ or CoreOS to follow? thanks!

For people who cannot click through, this is the qemu commit fixing it in qemu stable 7.2.1:
https://gitlab.com/mjt0k/qemu/-/commit/c45d10f6557b4fdae3e97f1326429bb85f5a8717
What we still need is a Fedora 38 CoreOS update that includes qemu 7.2.1. Its already in F38, but apparently not in a stamped CoreOS ostree update.
